title: The Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act, 1952
full_text: The Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act, 1952 राष्‍ट्रपतीयऔरउपराष्‍ट्रपतीयनिर्वाचनअधिनियम, 1952 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 1952-31 Act Number: 31 Enactment Date: 1952-03-14 Act Year: 1952 Short Title: The Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act, 1952 Hindi Title: राष्‍ट्रपतीयऔरउपराष्‍ट्रपतीयनिर्वाचनअधिनियम, 1952 Long Title: An Act to regulate certain matters relating to or connected with elections to the offices of President and Vice-President of India. Ministry: Ministry of Law and Justice Department: Legislative Department Enforcement Date: 14-03-1952 Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms TitleFilesFORM 1 FORM 2 FORM 3 FORM 4 FORM 5 FORM 6 FORM 7 TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Presidential and Vice-Presidential Elections Act, 1952 PART I PRELIMINARY PART II CONDUCT OF PRESIDENTIAL AND VICE-PRESIDENTIAL ELECTIONS PART III DISPUTES REGARDING ELECTIONS PART IV MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title. Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Returning Officer and his assistants. Section 4. Appointment of dates for nominations, etc. Show Related Subordinates Section 5. Public notice of election. Show Related Subordinates Section 5A. Nomination of candidates. Section 5B. Presentation of nomination papers and requirements for a valid nomination. Show Related Subordinates Section 5C. Deposit. Section 5D. Notice of nominations and the time and place for their scrutiny. Section 5E. Scrutiny of nominations. Show Related Subordinates Section 6. Withdrawal of candidature. Show Related Subordinates Section 7. Death of candidate before poll. Section 8. Procedure in contested and uncontested elections. Show Related Subordinates Section 9. Manner of voting at elections. Section 10. Counting of votes. Section 11. Declaration of results. Section 12. Report of the result. Show Related Subordinates Section 13. Definitions. Section 14. Authority to try election petitions. Section 14A. Presentation of petition. Section 15. Form of petitions, etc., and procedure. Section 16. Relief that may be claimed by the petitioner. Section 17. Orders of the Supreme Court. Section 18. Grounds for declaring the election of a returned candidate to be void. Section 19. Grounds for which a candidate other than the returned candidate may he declared to have been elected. Section 20. Transmission of orders to the Central Government and its publication Section 20A. Return or forfeiture of candidate‟s deposit. Section 21. Power to make rules. Show Related Subordinates Section 22. Maintenance of secrecy of voting. Show Related Subordinates Section 23. Jurisdiction of civil courts barred Schedule 1. THE SCHEDULE.
